verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Lunch
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

best.windFit: N 20 km/h · cross-onshore · poor wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 96 kJ, 10 s period, consistent 67/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean small window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 43 kJ, 10 s period, lully 15/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 10-13s at low tide is fast and technical at Supertubos / Molhe Leste.
ranked.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is helping, but the spot still looks serious.
warnings: 10-13s at low tide is fast and technical at Supertubos / Molhe Leste

photo.alt: Photo of Cantinho da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 107 kJ, 10 s period, consistent 71/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 10-13s at mid tide gives the sheltered bay workable wrap-around lines; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should help the bay stay organised.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size
